channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Comparing the 2012 and 2026 Gordie Howe Bridge Agreements
item.description: The article explains the differences between the 2012 Canada-Michigan bridge agreement and the 2026 Canada-U.S. pact for the Gordie Howe International Bridge. The 2012 deal focused on long-term toll r...

item.title: Terrorist Attack in Anantnag Market Kills J&K Policeman, First Since Pahalgam
item.description: A terrorist attack in Anantnag's Lal Chowk market killed J&K Police constable Ashiq Hussain Qureshi, marking the first such incident in Kashmir since the Pahalgam massacre in 2025. The attack occurred...

item.title: Tolu Arokodare's Protest Leads to Transfer to Fiorentina
item.description: Tolu Arokodare, Nigeria's international striker, caused a stir by ending his Wolverhampton Wanderers training session abruptly. He refused to leave the pitch, demanding a transfer to Fiorentina. This ...

item.title: Mine Trade pledges to increase Tanzanian participation in mining sector
item.description: The article highlights Tanzania Mine Trade's commitment to promoting Tanzanian involvement in the mining sector. It mentions the organization's efforts to create opportunities for local Tanzanians in ...

item.title: Two Honduran Couples Drown While Rescuing Distressed Swimmer in Ohio River
item.description: Two married couples from Honduras have died after attempting to rescue a distressed swimmer in the Scioto River near Columbus, Ohio. The incident occurred on Sunday when a group of five adults and two...

item.title: Nursing Protest in Uttarakhand: Aspirants Demand Recruitment Reforms
item.description: A group of nursing aspirants in Uttarakhand staged a protest outside the residence of Health Minister Subodh Uniyal in Dehradun, demanding year-wise recruitment based on seniority and other pending is...

item.title: CAF president Motsepe confirms no third term and backs Infantino for Fifa re-election
item.description: CAF president Patrice Motsepe has confirmed he will not seek a third term in office, stating that no one will persuade him to extend his tenure beyond two terms. During a media briefing in Sandton, Mo...

item.title: Father Sentenced to Life for Rape of His Daughters
item.description: A 52-year-old man has been handed a life sentence in the Malmesbury Regional Court for the sexual abuse of his three biological daughters. The convicted father, whose identity is protected to safeguar...

item.title: Yanga Faces New Season Challenges Without Key Players
item.description: The article discusses the Tanzanian football club Yanga's preparations for the upcoming season, highlighting the absence of key players such as Mzize, Job, and Pacome. These players have left the team...

item.title: Toronto Police Officer Linked to Project South Corruption Scandal Pleads Guilty
item.description: A former Toronto police constable, Derek McCormick, has pleaded guilty to theft and breach of trust charges related to supplying ‘burner phones’ from lost property to another officer involved in the P...

item.title: ACT Unveils Climate Strategy Beyond Net-Zero Targets
item.description: The Australian Capital Territory (ACT) government has announced a comprehensive climate strategy that extends beyond the traditional net-zero emissions target, focusing on building resilience against ...

item.title: Quantum Shuttle Mechanism Enhances Triplet Energy Transfer in Materials
item.description: Researchers at the Dalian Institute of Chemical Physics, Chinese Academy of Sciences, discovered a novel proton-assisted mechanism that significantly improves triplet energy transfer between quantum d...

item.title: Filipino Distrust in China Grows Amid West Philippine Sea Tensions
item.description: A recent survey by Octa Research reveals that 64% of Filipinos distrust China, a significant increase from 55% in March 2026. The survey, conducted from July 4 to 11, coincided with the Philippines' 1...

item.title: Proposed Restrictions on Transgender Athletes in School Sports
item.description: The article discusses proposed policy recommendations by the Center for American Progress regarding transgender athletes participating in school sports. Advocates argue that competitive advantages fro...

item.title: Court Inspects Ogun Meth Lab in ₦480bn Drug Case, Hearing Adjourned to July 24
item.description: The Federal High Court in Lagos conducted an inspection of a suspected methamphetamine production facility in Ogun State as part of the trial for 10 defendants accused of operating a clandestine ₦480 ...

item.title: Ontario Premier Doug Ford's Call to Block Oil and Potash Exports to U.S. Faces Cold Provincial Response
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford has once again proposed restricting oil and potash exports to the U.S. as a retaliatory measure against new tariffs, but the idea has faced strong opposition from Alberta and...

item.title: US President Commends Nigerian Leadership on Counter-Terrorism Efforts
item.description: US President Donald Trump has written to Nigerian President Bola Ahmed Tinubu, commending his decisive leadership in combating terrorism and addressing security challenges in Nigeria. The letter, disc...

item.title: South Gauteng DPP Andrew Chauke Reinstated After Inquiry Finds No Basis for Allegations
item.description: Retired Constitutional Court Justice Bess Nkabinde's inquiry concluded that South Gauteng Director of Public Prosecutions Advocate Andrew Chauke is fit to resume his duties. The decision follows a yea...

item.title: Mwigulu launches NBC Dodoma Marathon as thousands participate
item.description: The article reports on the launch of the NBC Dodoma Marathon, organized by Mwigulu, which is expected to attract a large number of participants. The event is set to take place in Dodoma, the capital o...

item.title: National Education Rights Movement Gains Momentum Following Student Protests
item.description: The article discusses the growing National Education Rights Movement in India, which has gained momentum following widespread student protests demanding better educational facilities and rights. Stude...
